_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
The AFDC database is a reference and navigation tool rather than a registration authority itself; it does not impose direct registration obligations on freight forwarders. However, it consolidates the registration and reporting obligations of the underlying federal programs it indexes.

**§45Z Clean Fuel Production Credit — Producer Registration:** Fuel producers (not freight forwarders as purchasers) must register with the IRS before production begins by filing **Form 637** under Activity Letter "CA" (SAF producer) or "CN" (non-SAF clean fuel producer), or both. The IRS released Notice 2024-49 (May 31, 2024) providing the registration procedure, and published FAQs in July 2024. A taxpayer cannot claim the §45Z credit unless registered at the time of production. Registration is a prerequisite, not a periodic renewal obligation, but producers must maintain compliance throughout the credit period (January 1, 2025 – December 31, 2029). The workspace's role as a fuel purchaser means it has no direct Form 637 obligation, but it should request proof of supplier registration as a procurement due-diligence step. Producers file **Form 7218** (Clean Fuel Production Credit) annually with their income tax return; the IRS publishes annual emissions-rate tables (most recently Notice 2025-11) that determine the per-gallon credit value.

**§45W Commercial Clean Vehicle Credit — Seller/Dealer Reporting:** Although the §45W credit deadline has passed (September 30, 2025), sellers of clean commercial vehicles were required to register with IRS and submit time-of-sale reports via the Energy Credits Online portal. The portal closed for new user registration on September 30, 2025, but remains open for previously registered users to submit outstanding time-of-sale reports and corrections.

**State-level registration:** The AFDC database indexes state-level alternative fuel vehicle (AFV) acquisition and exemption reporting under the federal Energy Policy Act (EPAct) Section 701 State and Alternative Fuel Provider (S&FP) program. Entities that qualify as "alternative fuel providers" or "state fleets" under EPAct may have annual AFV acquisition reporting obligations to DOE; the workspace should confirm whether its vehicle fleet volume or fuel-providing activities trigger S&FP program applicability.

**Data fields the workspace must collect for potential credit participation:** For any SAF supply contract, the workspace should collect from suppliers: fuel type (SAF or non-SAF), feedstock type and country of origin, IRS Form 637 registration letter date and activity letter designation, annual production volume, and ASTM standard under which SAF is produced (required per Notice 2024-49).

*Source: IRS, Clean Fuel Production Credit Registration FAQs, IRS.gov, July 2024. https://www.irs.gov/newsroom/frequently-asked-questions-about-applying-for-registration-for-the-clean-fuel-production-credit-under-ss-45z; AFDC, Clean Fuel Production Credit Registration Guidance, afdc.energy.gov. https://afdc.energy.gov/laws/13450*
